- Auto-fallback: models in production must have fallback policy to lower verbosity or shorter model variant on >0.1% token-anomaly rate over 1 hour.
- Alert thresholds (first-line):
  - token_anomaly_rate > 0.1% -> paging to oncall (P3)
  - avg_billable_tokens increase > 8% vs 7-day baseline -> notify cost-ops Slack channel
  - decode_tokens P99 increase > 15% -> create auto-ticket for Applied ML team

Quantization and Runtime Considerations:
- When evaluating quant_profiles, record both billable_tokens and observed latency delta. Some quantization paths may change tokenization due to mixed-precision decoding heuristics; include tokenizer-DIFF tests to detect any unintended token boundary shifts.
- Run the quant_profile through the "KV-cache hit-rate probe": measure cache hit-rate across synthetic session sequences. A drop >5 percentage points should block promotion.

Regression Triage Playbook (token regressions):
1) Detect: alert livedetected by metric pipeline (token_anomaly_rate or TEI delta).
2) Reproduce locally: use the failing slice seed and EvalRunner to replay top 200 anomalous requests against baseline and candidate.
3) Binary isolation: switch out one axis at a time (decoder impl, tokenizer version, quant_profile, sampling params) to localize cause.
4) Blame candidate or runtime: run targeted A/B shadow runs to determine if the behavior is model-intrinsic or introduced by runtime (batcher/quant).
5) If model-intrinsic: open a model-regression issue and attach seeds, prompt examples, and per-request token traces (prefix, decode tokens).
6) If runtime-intrinsic: create an infra ticket and include kernel logs, quant config, and GPU distribution topology.
7) Rollback criteria: if TEI delta > 0.05 with adverse quality delta or token_anomaly_rate > 0.3% sustained for >30 minutes, perform rollback to previous promoted variant.

Operator Playbook (oncall steps):
1) Acknowledge alert in Ops channel and run the quick-repro script: evalctl replay --slice <name> --top 200 --variant candidate
2) Check Grafana panels (ER-TOKEN-001) for correlated CPU/GPU spikes and queue latency.
3) If auto-fallback did not trigger, manually toggle fallback route to lower-verbosity variant and monitor TEI.
4) Notify Applied ML and Runtime teams and create incident page with initial findings.

Ownership and SLAs:
- Applied ML: primary owner for benchmark definitions, TEI weights, and quality scoring (sla: 2 business days for Triage ack).
- Runtime / Serving: primary owner for quant_profile validation and KV-cache behavior (sla: 8 business hours for critical infra tickets).
- Cost-Ops: owner for cost-related dashboards and billing integration (sla: 3 business days for cost anomalies).

Appendix A — Example artifact (JSON metric sample):
{
  "request_id": "req_20260215_0001",
  "model_variant": "redwood-open-2.1",
  "prefix_tokens": 42,
  "decode_tokens": 128,
  "billable_tokens": 170,
  "start_ts": "2026-02-15T14:01:02Z",
  "end_ts": "2026-02-15T14:01:02.200Z"
}

Appendix B — Common troubleshooting checklist:
- Verify tokenizer versions match between baseline and candidate.
- Replay seeds deterministically (seed + RNG path).
- Check sampling param drift (temperature/top-p) in request logs.
- Inspect KV-cache hit counters for session workloads.
- Run tiny-batch vs large-batch runs to observe batching amplification effects.

How to use this playbook:
- When creating a new model promotion PR, include a Token-Economy section with TEI numbers, sampling slices, and any discovered token anomalies.
- For runtime changes (quant or batching), run the token-economy baseline and verify no TEI regressions before deployment to canary.

Contacts:
- Applied ML oncall: #applied-ml-oncall (Slack)
- Runtime SRE: #runtime-sre (Slack)
- Cost Ops owner: cost-ops@redwood.ai

Revision history:
- 2026-01-12: Created by Maya Chen (initial draft).
- 2026-02-20: Reviewed and published; clarified TEI thresholds and rollback criteria.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook

Summary:
This handbook defines a cross-cutting, repeatable process to take an anchored probe (a stable, annotated test case or cohort) from lab validation through shadowing, canary, and full production promotion while accounting for quantization profiles, runtime priming/kv-cache behavior, and regression triage. It codifies ownership, measurable quality guardrails, telemetry signals, and concrete rollback criteria so production promotions are fast, auditable, and safe.

Purpose and scope:
- Purpose: Reduce time-to-confidence for model, quant, and prompt changes by standardizing the anchor->shadow->canary->prod path and embedding regression fast-paths.
- Scope: Applies to: eval harness probes, prompt families used in production prompts, model onboarding changes (weights/architectural variants), quantization profile rollouts, and cache/batching runtime changes. Does not replace product-level A/B experiments; it complements them for inference-level quality safety.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...] - Scope: Applies to: eval harness probes, prompt families used in production prompts, model onboarding changes (weights/architectural variants), quantization profile rollouts, and cache/batching runtime changes. Does not replace product-level A/B experiments; it complements them for inference-level quality safety.


Definitions (brief):
- Anchor (anchored probe): a curated, annotated instance or small cohort representing a critical production slice with ground truth or high-fidelity human labels.
- Shadow mesh: controlled parallel traffic to a candidate variant for telemetry capture without affecting end users.
- Canary: small percentage of live traffic routed to candidate with automatic rollback triggers.
- Probe score: composite metric across label-fidelity, semantic-stability, and latency/cost delta.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...] - Probe score: composite metric across label-fidelity, semantic-stability, and latency/cost delta.


Roles and ownership:
- Probe Owner: creates and maintains anchored probes; usually Evaluation or Model Onboarding engineer. (Primary contact)
- Shadow/Canary Owner: Platform/Serving runtime engineer who configures traffic and telemetry ingestion.
- Quality Steward: Applied-ML engineer responsible for guardrails, thresholds, and triage coordination.
- Incident Lead: SRE/Oncall when canary triggers a rollback.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...] - Incident Lead: SRE/Oncall when canary triggers a rollback.


Anchored Probe Promotion Flow (high-level steps):
1) Local lab validation (Probe Owner)
   - Run anchored probe across baseline and candidate (N>=20 deterministic seeds where applicable).
   - Verify tokenization parity and context-priming equivalence (check token counts and embeddings).
   - Produce probe report: differences in correctness, hallucination indicators, and token-level attention anomalies.

2) Quant/Runtime compatibility check (Model Onboarding + Platform)
   - Test candidate with each target quant profile (FP16, int8/awq-like, mixed) in an isolated sandbox.
   - Validate KV cache behavior: replicate cold-to-warm transitions and check for prefix-shift regressions.
   - Minimum acceptance: no >3% relative degradation on probe score for safety-critical slices; configurable by steward.

3) Shadow mesh (Shadow Owner)
   - Deploy candidate as shadow for a period (24–72 hours typical) across representative traffic.
   - Capture telemetry: response text, token-level timings, model confidence proxies, cost-per-token, and embedding drift.
   - Run automated evaluators (fast heuristics + sampling for human annotation).

4) Micro-canary (Shadow/Canary Owner)
   - Route 0.5–2% production traffic for 2–12 hours depending on SLA risk.
   - Enable early-warning sentinels (see "Telemetry and Alerts" section).
   - If any sentinel trips, enact immediate automatic rollback and notify Incident Lead.

5) Full rollout (Platform + Product)
   - Gradual ramp with step increases and hold windows (e.g., 5% -> 25% -> 50% -> 100%).
   - Monitor guardrails and business KPIs; require sign-off from Quality Steward at 50% and 100%.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...]    - Monitor guardrails and business KPIs; require sign-off from Quality Steward at 50% and 100%.


Lab validation and experiment design (concrete):
- Sampling: stratified sampling across tenant-critical cohorts, long-context slices (>1024 tokens), and rare-intent buckets. Maintain minimum sample sizes per cohort: 200 for high-volume slices, 50 for edge slices.
- Multi-seed determinism: run 5 deterministic seeds for stochastic decoding to estimate variance; compute inter-seed BLEU/ROUGE/semantic-similarity spread.
- Human audit: 10% sample (min 30 examples) annotated blind for fidelity checks when probe score delta is within the noise band.
- Statistical flags: use bootstrap CI on probe-score; if 95% CI crosses production baseline, mark for manual review.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...] - Statistical flags: use bootstrap CI on probe-score; if 95% CI crosses production baseline, mark for manual review.


Quantization and runtime checklist (sandbox tests):
- Verify numeric equivalence tolerances across outputs: token-level edit distance and embedding cosine >0.98 for non-degenerate slices.
- Run throughput and tail-latency profile with batching turned on/off; quantify delta in p95/p99.
- Confirm memory/KV cache hit-rates and eviction patterns remain within expected range (document expected baselines per model family).
- Example commands: 
  # Run quant-sanity harness against anchored probe
  ./harness/run_probe.sh --model candidate-v2 --quant awq-8 --probe anchored/probe-123.yaml --seeds 5 --output report.json

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
section_header: Shadow and canary telemetry (signals and thresholds)
text:
[...]   ./harness/run_probe.sh --model candidate-v2 --quant awq-8 --probe anchored/probe-123.yaml --seeds 5 --output report.json


Shadow and canary telemetry (signals and thresholds):
- Primary signals (immediate auto-roll triggers):
  - Probe score drop > 7% absolute on any safety-critical anchor.
  - Hallucination sentinel rate increase > 3x baseline on anchor cohort.
  - Latency p99 increase > 200ms over baseline AND prod error-rate increase > 0.2%.
  - Tokenization mismatch errors (detected via parity-checker).
- Secondary signals (alert + review):
  - Embedding drift (cosine decrease > 0.06 average across cohort).
  - Cost delta > 30% per 1k tokens for the same quality tier.
  - Increase in diversity or variance of multi-seed outputs beyond historical variance.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
section_header: Telemetry and alerting configuration (example table-like spec)
text:
[...]   - Increase in diversity or variance of multi-seed outputs beyond historical variance.


Telemetry and alerting configuration (example table-like spec):
- Name: probe_score_anchor
  - Source: eval-harness/ingest
  - Aggregation: hourly, cohort-level
  - Alert: critical if delta <= -0.07 on safety_cohort over 1 hour
- Name: kv_cache_eviction_rate
  - Source: runtime/metrics
  - Aggregation: 5m
  - Alert: warn if > baseline+15%
(Implement these as concrete monitors in the dashboards team.)

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
section_header: Regression triage fast path (when a canary trips)
text:
[...] (Implement these as concrete monitors in the dashboards team.)


Regression triage fast path (when a canary trips):
1) Capture snapshot: commit hash for model, quant profile, runtime kernel, routing policy, and the exact probe batch IDs.
2) Run automated bisect: compare candidate vs last-known-good on anchor and adjacent slices; report minimal-diff examples.
3) If bisect narrows to quant variant: reproduce in quant sandbox and attempt 1-step quant fallback (e.g., AWQ->FP16) in shadow to validate hypothesis.
4) If bisect implicates prompt/template change or priming behavior: run context-trimming stability test (see appendix).
5) If not resolved in 60 minutes, escalate to Incident Lead and enact rollback while preserving full telemetry.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...] 5) If not resolved in 60 minutes, escalate to Incident Lead and enact rollback while preserving full telemetry.


Investigation playbook (detailed steps):
- Step A (Automated evidence):
  1) Pull the probe report and the diff bundle (diff-on-token, top-k logits, embedding pairs).
  2) Run short-form statistical analysis: sign tests, paired-difference bootstrap.
- Step B (Hypothesis generation)
  1) Check quantation: compare per-layer quant-residuals and checksum mismatches.
  2) Check for tokenization or context-length boundary shifts.
  3) If outputs differ but token-level logits similar, suspect decoding/temperature/seed drift.
- Step C (Human verification)
  1) Triage sample to annotators flagged as "calibration fast-queue".
  2) Annotator delivers binary pass/fail plus verbal explanation within 2 hours.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
section_header: Promotion readiness checklist (must all be true unless steward approves exceptions)
text:
[...]   2) Annotator delivers binary pass/fail plus verbal explanation within 2 hours.


Promotion readiness checklist (must all be true unless steward approves exceptions):
- [ ] Probe regression tests: pass for all safety_anchors (N deterministic seeds, probe-score delta within noise).
- [ ] Quant compatibility: candidate validated on target quant profiles.
- [ ] Runtime smoke: cold/warm cache, batching, and priming smoke tests passed.
- [ ] Shadow telemetry: no critical sentinel trips for 24h.
- [ ] Canary health: stable for minimum canary window (configurable by risk tier).
- [ ] Documentation: model card, quant profile manifest, guardrail config committed to repo.

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...] - [ ] Documentation: model card, quant profile manifest, guardrail config committed to repo.


Rollback and postmortem norms:
- Automatic rollback should preserve the candidate environment for a minimum of 48 hours for postmortem reproduction.
- Postmortem must include: timeline, primary evidence (diffs, telemetry snapshots), root-cause hypothesis with attempted mitigations, and a remediation plan.
- Remediation owner assigned within 24 hours; follow-up review at next Governance review (weekly).

Appendix A — context-trimming stability test (quick recipe):
- Purpose: detect regressions caused by early-token priming or KV-cache shifts for long-context prompts.
- Procedure: for each anchor with >2048 tokens, generate truncated variants: keep-last-1024, keep-first-1024, sliding-window(512, stride=256). Compare model outputs and compute the stability index. Flag if stability index < 0.92.

Appendix B — exemplary JSON manifest (promotion metadata):
{
  "candidate_model": "candidate-v2",
  "commit": "abc123def",
  "quant_profile": "awq-8b",
  "probe_anchor_ids": ["probe-123", "probe-77"],
  "shadow_window": "2026-02-15T00:00Z/2026-02-18T00:00Z"
}

Appendix C — quick CLI snippets:
- Run shadow deployment (example):
  rwoodctl deploy shadow --model candidate-v2 --route-selector "tenant:sampled" --telemetry-sink eval-harness:shadow-bucket --duration 72h
- Trigger automated bisect (example):
  eval-bisect --left baseline:2026-02-01 --right candidate:candidate-v2 --anchors anchored/probe-*.yaml --out bisect-report.json

title: Cross-cutting Anchored Probe to Production Handbook
text:
[...]   eval-bisect --left baseline:2026-02-01 --right candidate:candidate-v2 --anchors anchored/probe-*.yaml --out bisect-report.json


Related pages and references:
- See the evaluation mesh and workload fingerprinting guide for sampling heuristics.
- See quant-profile-synthesis playbook for detailed quant safety checks.

Contact and stewardship:
- Primary: Aisha Kumar (applied-ml)
- Shadow/Canary: Marcus Chen (platform)
- Quality Steward: Lena Ortiz (applied-ml)

Revision history:
- 2026-02-10: Created (A. Kumar)
- 2026-02-25: Added canary sentinel thresholds and rollback timing (L. Ortiz)
- 2026-03-01: Finalized promotion checklist and namespaces for telemetry sources (M. Chen)

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite

Overview

This page describes the Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark (APCB): a small, production-focused probe suite designed to measure and track priming/anchoring robustness across model variants, quantization profiles, and KV-cache behaviors. APCB is intended as a low-cost continuous probe that can run in shadow and canary lanes of the eval harness to catch early regressions in few-shot priming fidelity and primer decay across contexts.

Purpose and goals

- Provide a repeatable, small-footprint benchmark (50–200 probes) suitable for continuous runs.
- Measure anchor retention (how long a primer continues to influence outputs) and primer-channel isolation (whether the primer leaks into unrelated generations).
- Surface interactions between priming, quantization, and KV-cache reuse that could cause semantic drift or hallucination.
- Produce actionable signals with clear triage steps and remediation recommendations.

Scope

- Applied to text-only and single-turn few-shot prompts (1–5 examples). Not a multimodal benchmark.
- Runs against: hosted model variants, dedicated instances (representative instance types), and quantized shadow artifacts.
- Intended for both offline eval harness scheduling and lightweight in-production shadow sampling.

Design principles

1. Compact: keep the probe set small (~100 probes) while covering key failure modes.
2. Anchored: each probe explicitly includes an anchor section (highly directive text) and a neutral test segment to check anchor spill.
3. Diversity: include multiple prompt styles (instruction, QA, roleplay, function-call templates).
4. Deterministic scoring when possible: use token-level and semantic similarity metrics plus human-calibrated thresholds.

Probe taxonomy

- Anchor-retention probes: measure how many tokens/turns the primer continues to influence when context length increases.
- Anchor-leak probes: detect primer words or intent appearing in unrelated outputs.
- Function-call anchoring probes: check parameter name/value adherence after priming.
- Stochasticity probes: measure variance across seeds and streaming starts.

Probe synthesis and dataset

1. Seed corpus: 250 canonical seeds derived from real workloads (support, search snippets, chat intents). Seeds are anonymized and stripped of PII.
2. Primer types: directive ("Always answer in bullet points"), persona ("You are a helpful historian"), safety ("Do not provide legal advice"), and structural (function call scaffolding).
3. For each seed, generate 3–4 probe variants combining primer type and prompt style.

Sampling rules

- Shuffle probes per run and cap to N=100 per continuous run.
- Include deterministic random seed for reproducibility in the eval harness.
- Run both cold-start (no KV cache) and warm-start (KV cache primed with a short history) variants.

Metrics and scoring

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
[...] Metrics and scoring


Primary metrics (numeric):
- Anchor Retention Index (ARI): fraction of probes where the primary anchored instruction persists at specified context offsets (0, +50, +200 tokens). Values 0.0–1.0.
- Anchor Leakage Rate (ALR): percent of probes where anchor tokens or anchor intent appear in a mismatched output segment.
- Function-call Fidelity (FCF): percent of function-call probes that return the correct parameter structure and canonical values.
- Seed Variance (SV): standard deviation of ARI across 5 seeds (captures stochastic instability).

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
[...] - Seed Variance (SV): standard deviation of ARI across 5 seeds (captures stochastic instability).


Auxiliary signals (diagnostic):
- Token overlap counts between anchor and output.
- Edit distance to expected canonical answer.
- Embedding cosine similarity to anchored answer (for semantic retention).

Acceptance thresholds (example table)

| Metric | Acceptable | Warning | Fail |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| ARI (0 token offset) | >= 0.92 | 0.80–0.91 | < 0.80 |
| ARI (+200 tokens) | >= 0.78 | 0.65–0.77 | < 0.65 |
| ALR | <= 0.04 | 0.05–0.10 | > 0.10 |
| FCF | >= 0.95 | 0.85–0.94 | < 0.85 |
| SV | <= 0.03 | 0.03–0.08 | > 0.08 |

Note: thresholds should be tuned per-model family and per-quantization profile. Use this table as a starting guardrail rather than absolutes.

Integration with eval harness and CI

- Harness job: a lightweight job runner named apcb-compact-runner. It supports parallelism=8 and artifacts exported as JSON L2 summaries.
- Schedule: run every 4 hours in shadow for high-traffic models; nightly for low-traffic.
- CI gate: failing the Fail thresholds for two consecutive runs triggers a blocking ticket and auto-notification to model-onboarding and serving-runtime on-call.

CLI example (eval harness adapter)

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
section_header: Sample eval job YAML (for harness scheduler)
text:
[...] CLI example (eval harness adapter)


# Run APCB against a model variant
apcb-runner --model redwood-gptx-1b-qa --profiles cold,warm --seed 42 --limit 100 --output s3://apcb/artifacts/${RUN_ID}.json

Sample eval job YAML (for harness scheduler):

job_name: apcb-compact-nightly
schedule: nightly
parallelism: 8
profile: [cold, warm]
seeds: 42,43,44
limit: 100
notify_on: [warning, fail]

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
[...] notify_on: [warning, fail]


Artifact schema (short):
- run_id
- model_variant
- profile
- probe_id
- metrics: {ari, alr, fcf, sv}
- raw_output_text
- tokens_for_anchor

(Artifacts must remain JSON lines compliant for downstream triage tools.)

Triage flow and remediation playbook

1. Automated triage: the harness computes delta against baseline (previous stable variant).
2. If Fail thresholds hit: open regression ticket with tags [apcb, regression, model:<variant>]. Attach full run artifact and delta summary.
3. Quick manual checks (Owner: model-onboarding):
   - Re-run failing probes with 5 different seeds and both cold/warm profiles.
   - Compare against unquantized reference to isolate quantization impact.
   - Inspect tokenization differences (special tokens, subword splits).
4. Escalation matrix:
   - If isolated to quant profile -> engage quant team and apply rollback to previous quant profile.
   - If isolated to KV cache behavior -> route to serving-runtime with run_id and capture trace.
   - If model variant-regression across all profiles -> open rollback and coordinate release blocking.

Remediation suggestions (common fixes)

- Primer rewrite / anchoring strengthening: add explicit delimiting tokens or anchor markers to primers.
- Quant fallback: temporarily route production traffic to unquantized or higher-precision variant for affected route.
- KV-cache priming hygiene: truncate reused context and inject anchor sentinel tokens at start of primed sections.
- Function-call schema tightening: enforce strict schema validators and add replayed function-call unit tests to the evaluator.

Ownership and runbook contact list

- Maintainer (apcb steward): Sofia Martinez (applied-ml) — apcb-maintain@redwood.ai
- Model-onboarding lead: Arjun Patel — model-onboarding@redwood.ai
- Quant team pager: #quant-ops (Slack) and quant-oncall@redwood.ai
- Serving-runtime oncall: via SRE rota (see /eng-serving-runtime/runbooks)

Versioning and baseline management

- Baselines: keep a baseline per major model family (e.g., gptx-1, gptx-2) and per quantization profile. Baselines are immutable and stored in the artifact registry with tags baseline/YYYYMMDD.
- Baseline promotion: after a canary passes APCB for 72 hours with no Fail alerts, promotion is recorded with a baseline tag and release notes.

Privacy and dataset handling

- All probe seeds are scrubbed for PII and customer-sensitive terms before inclusion.
- Long-term retention: raw outputs retained for 90 days, aggregated metrics retained for 3 years.

Appendix: sample primer and probe examples

1) Directive anchor (anchor-retention probe)

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
[...] 
1) Directive anchor (anchor-retention probe)

Primer:
"You MUST answer in numbered steps, and do not include any examples."
Seed test: "Explain how to rotate an API key."
Expected: numbered-step answer and no example blocks.

2) Function-call anchor

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
[...] 
2) Function-call anchor

Primer:
"When answering, return a JSON object matching the schema: {\"action\":string, \"params\":{}}"
Seed test: "Create a function call to schedule an email to user@example.com next Tuesday."
Expected: JSON with action=\"schedule_email\" and params.email present.

3) Anchor-leak probe

title: Anchor + Primer Compact Benchmark and Production Probe Suite
text:
[...] 
3) Anchor-leak probe

Primer:
"You are a customer service agent for Acme Roofing. Avoid mentioning Acme if unrelated."
Seed test: "Summarize the difference between asphalt and metal shingles."
Pass condition: no mention of Acme in unrelated context.

Appendix: recommended dashboard widgets

- ARI time-series (0/50/200 token offsets) with model/quantization profile breakdown.
- ALR heatmap by probe type and model family.
- Daily FCF pass rate with quick links to failing probes.

Notes and next steps

- We recommend running APCB as part of any quantization rollout and as a steady-state shadow job for high-volume models.
- Roadmap items: expand to multimodal primers, integrate differential privacy noise-aware variants, and add automated primer-rewrite suggestions into the remediation pipeline.

Document change log

- 2026-11-15: Initial draft (S. Martinez).
- 2026-12-20: Added CI gating and artifact schema (Arjun Patel review).
- 2027-02-03: Tuned thresholds and added KV-cache warm-start profile (Maya Chen review).

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding

Summary:
This document defines the Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol used when introducing a new model (or a major model variant) into Redwood’s serving and eval pipelines. The protocol standardizes: initial sample selection, staged evaluation rounds, statistical stability tests, quantization compatibility checks, and automated gating for promotion to shadow or canary stages.

Scope and intent:
- Applies to any new model version or architecture introduced to Redwood’s Hosted, Dedicated, or Private deployments.
- Intended for the first 0–30 days after a model is first integrated into our eval-harness.
- Not a substitute for safety or policy reviews — those must be executed in parallel per the safety-sandbox process.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - Not a substitute for safety or policy reviews — those must be executed in parallel per the safety-sandbox process.


Goals:
1) Obtain representative, reproducible quality metrics with minimal cost.
2) Detect early regressions vs. production baseline and historical models.
3) Ensure quantization/latency/kv-cache interactions are covered in the cold-start window.
4) Provide a deterministic gating signal for promotion to longer-running shadow evaluation.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] 4) Provide a deterministic gating signal for promotion to longer-running shadow evaluation.


Ownership and roles:
- Author: Amina Chowdhury (Applied ML) — maintains protocol definitions and templates.
- Eval-runner Owner: team that operates the eval-harness (eng-applied-ml).
- Onboarding Owner: model on-call / model steward from Research or Inference.
- Security Reviewer: security-and-compliance for privacy-sensitive sampling.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - Security Reviewer: security-and-compliance for privacy-sensitive sampling.


Key definitions and metrics:
- Bootstrap round: one discrete evaluation pass against a sampled workload slice. Typical rounds: R0..R3.
- Cold-start window: first 7 days (configurable up to 30d) after first integration.
- Stability Index (SI): normalized variance measure of primary quality metric across repeated rounds. Lower is more stable.
- Regret Rate: proportion of samples where the new model performs below baseline threshold.
- Latency SLO breach rate: percent tokens/requests exceeding configured latency SLO for the deployment tier.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - Latency SLO breach rate: percent tokens/requests exceeding configured latency SLO for the deployment tier.


Sampling strategy (high level):
1) Seed selection: combine stratified production slices + synthetic stress probes. Use stratification keys: product-route, language, user-intent, sequence-length-bucket.
2) Warmup quota: reserve 20% of eval budget for short context / prefix-heavy samples to exercise KV cache path.
3) Edge coverage: 10% of samples are adversarial/edge prompts from the edgecase-probe inventory.
4) Randomization: apply deterministic seed per-model (model-id + date) to allow replayable sampling.

Recommended sizes: (table representation)
- R0 (sanity): 200 samples (fast), focus on smoke checks and integration.
- R1 (bootstrap): 2,000 samples, stratified coverage across 5 primary slices.
- R2 (stability): 10,000 samples, multi-seed (n=3) aggregation.
- R3 (safety/quant): 1,000 targeted stress probes including quantized replay.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - R3 (safety/quant): 1,000 targeted stress probes including quantized replay.


Example sampling config (YAML):

sampling_config:
  model_id: "new-model-2026-03"
  seed: 123456
  rounds:
    - name: R0_sanity
      size: 200
      slices: ["prod-top-10-routes", "sanity-templates"]
    - name: R1_bootstrap
      size: 2000
      stratify_by: ["route","lang","len_bucket"]
    - name: R2_stability
      size: 10000
      multi_seed: 3
    - name: R3_quant_compat
      size: 1000
      probes: ["kv-cache", "quant_roundtrip", "streaming_fncall"]

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...]       probes: ["kv-cache", "quant_roundtrip", "streaming_fncall"]


Procedures (step-by-step):
1) Integration and smoke (Owner: Onboarding Owner)
   a) Deploy model artifact to eval-harness using reproducible image/tag.
   b) Run R0; verify end-to-end integration, tokenization parity, and no fatal errors.
   c) Confirm logging hooks (token-level telemetry, model_version tag) are flowing to the sandbox metrics cluster.

2) Bootstrap evaluation (Owner: Eval-runner)
   a) Execute R1 with the sampling_config. Capture metrics: acceptability_score, bleu/bert_score (where applicable), SI, latency_p50/p95, token_cost_estimate.
   b) Aggregate results and compute Regret Rate vs baseline_model using pre-registered baseline_id.
   c) If Regret Rate > 5% OR SI > 0.08, create an automated triage issue and stop further automatic promotion.

3) Stability sweep (Owner: Applied ML/Onboarding)
   a) Run R2 across multi-seeds. Compute SI and confidence intervals for primary metrics.
   b) If metric variance decreases below configured thresholds (SI <= 0.05), mark as stable; else, require additional data or targeted prompt repairs.

4) Quantization compatibility (Owner: Runtime + Applied ML)
   a) Run R3 using quantized artifacts (int8/int4 profiles) and hybrid-run modes.
   b) Run quantized-replay-orchestrator to compare floating-point outputs to quantized outputs. Report: accuracy_delta, token_pattern-divergence, kv-cache-hits-diff.
   c) Block promotion if accuracy_delta on critical slices > 1.5% or if streaming toolcall regressions observed.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...]    c) Block promotion if accuracy_delta on critical slices > 1.5% or if streaming toolcall regressions observed.


Gating and promotion matrix:
- Gate to shadow: pass R0 + R1 with Regret Rate <= 3% and Latency SLO breach <= 1%
- Gate to canary: stability confirmed (R2) AND quant compat verified (R3) AND no critical safety flags
- Manual review required for any of: high SI, privacy-sensitive sample failures, or high-cost per-token delta

Statistical tests and thresholds:
- Use bootstrap resampling (n=1,000) to compute 95% CI on primary metrics.
- SI = sqrt(Var(metric))/mean_metric; require SI <= 0.05 for promotion beyond canary.
- For paired comparisons vs baseline, use Wilcoxon signed-rank if non-normal; require p < 0.05 for claiming superiority.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - For paired comparisons vs baseline, use Wilcoxon signed-rank if non-normal; require p < 0.05 for claiming superiority.


Telemetry and instrumentation checklist:
- Event tags: model_id, eval_round, sample_id, slice_key, seed_id, response_hash.
- Metrics emitted: primary_quality_metric, latency_ms, tokens_generated, kv_cache_hit, quant_mode, seed.
- Traces: sample-level traces stored for failing samples with full prompt + response + trace_id.

Automation and CI integration:
- CI job: onboarding/coldstart/<model_id> triggers R0 and R1 on PR to model-integration repo.
- Nightly job: schedule R2 runs for any model in cold-start window until stability achieved or 30d elapsed.
- Alerting: failing gating creates a ticket with triage tags: [eval-fail, coldstart, model_id].

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - Alerting: failing gating creates a ticket with triage tags: [eval-fail, coldstart, model_id].


Failure modes and triage notes:
- Symptom: high Latency p95 with normal p50 -> likely sequence-length distribution mismatch; inspect len_bucket distribution and KV cache warmup fraction.
- Symptom: large accuracy_delta only in quantized runs -> check quant-profile (per-workload-quant-tuner) and retry with hybrid-quant.
- Symptom: high Regret Rate on a single route -> isolate route slice, run focused R3 stress probe, and open targeted prompt surfacing task.

Reporting and handoffs:
- Standard deliverables after R2: evaluation report (PDF), failing-sample manifest (CSV), dashboard link, and triage tickets.
- Handoff to Reliability: if latency SLO breach persists at canary, open canary-blocker and assign to eng-serving-runtime.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - Handoff to Reliability: if latency SLO breach persists at canary, open canary-blocker and assign to eng-serving-runtime.


Appendices:
A. Example CLI to run a round (eval-harness runner)

# Run bootstrap round 1
redwood-eval run --model new-model-2026-03 --round R1_bootstrap --out results/R1_new-model-2026-03.json

B. Minimal result schema (JSON lines)
{
  "sample_id": "s-1234",
  "route": "generate-summary",
  "lang": "en",
  "prompt": "<text>",
  "response": "<text>",
  "primary_metric": 0.83,
  "latency_ms": 120,
  "kv_cache_hit": true
}

C. Quick checklist for model stewards:
- Verify instrumentation tags are present in first-run logs.
- Confirm synthetic stress probes cover streaming/function-call paths.
- Validate quantization artifacts with runtime team before R3.

title: Cold-start Bootstrap Sampling Protocol for New Model Onboarding
text:
[...] - Validate quantization artifacts with runtime team before R3.


Related documents and references:
- Inference readiness matrix (see related_pages)
- Quant-profile synthesis playbook
- Edgecase probe generator

Revision history:
- 2025-11-02: initial draft and internal review.
- 2026-01-10: added SI definition, gating thresholds tuned after pilot.
- 2026-02-14: formalized quantization gating and automation CI jobs.

Contact:
- For questions, open a PR on the eval-harness repo and tag @amina or ping #applied-ml on Slack.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook

Summary:
This playbook describes the Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator (TSPO): an operational pattern and reference implementation for safely sampling heavy-tailed tenant traffic, synthesizing privacy-safe probes for low-volume tenants, and projecting cost/impact prior to promotion to production evaluation or qual gates. TSPO addresses two recurrent issues: (1) noisy or sparse telemetry from rare tenants causing undercoverage of regressions, and (2) runaway evaluation costs when naive sampling pulls long-tail traffic at full fidelity.

Goals and non-goals:
- Goals:
  - Provide deterministic per-tenant sampling rules that preserve failure signal for heavy-tailed distributions while bounding eval spend to a configurable budget.
  - Allow automated generation of synthesized probes that mimic tenant context shape for low-volume tenants, preserving privacy and reducing data movement.
  - Expose cost-projection and acceptance thresholds to enable safe canary promotion.
- Non-goals:
  - This playbook does not replace primary production telemetry (use SRE runbooks for production incident capture).
  - It is not intended for synthetic adversarial testing (use adversarial-probe-suite workflows).

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...]   - It is not intended for synthetic adversarial testing (use adversarial-probe-suite workflows).


Definitions:
- Tenant-tail: Tenants that contribute <X% of total request volume but account for disproportionate slice-specific failures or latency tails.
- Synth-probe: A synthetic probe (input + expected metrics) generated from metadata + privacy-preserving aggregates to approximate a tenant workload.
- Evaluation fidelity: The level of runtime parity with production (e.g., full model weights on Dedicated vs quantized shadow).

SLOs and Budget Constraints:
- Coverage SLO: 95% of tenant slices with historical failure-rate > 2x baseline must be represented in weekly probe sets.
- Cost SLO (eval budget): TSPO sampling must not consume more than 6% of monthly eval credits per service.
- Latency SLO for shadow probes: end-to-end shadow request <= 3x production p95 for monitoring slices.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...] - Latency SLO for shadow probes: end-to-end shadow request <= 3x production p95 for monitoring slices.


Architecture overview:
- Components:
  1) Sampler service (sampler-tspo): computes per-tenant sampling probability using the TailScore algorithm (see sampling algorithm). Runs in eval-harness fleet and emits sample decisions to Kafka topic eval.sampler.decisions.
  2) Synthesizer (synth-probe-gen): consumes sampled tenant metadata and produces privacy-anchored synth-probes; stores artifacts in Probe Registry (S3://probe-registry/tspo/).
  3) Orchestrator (probe-orch): schedules probes through the shadow mesh, enforces budget gating, and records telemetry to eval-metrics/time-series.
  4) Cost model (tspo-cost-estimator): offline service producing per-probe cost estimates and monthly projection reports.
- Flow:
  1. Sampler emits decisions -> 2. Synthesizer produces probes for decisions marked synthable -> 3. Orchestrator schedules probes (respecting rate-limits) -> 4. Results recorded & cost estimator updates projections -> 5. Alerting if coverage or budget thresholds are violated.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...]   1. Sampler emits decisions -> 2. Synthesizer produces probes for decisions marked synthable -> 3. Orchestrator schedules probes (respecting rate-limits) -> 4. Results recorded & cost estimator updates projections -> 5. Alerting if coverage or budget thresholds are violated.


Sampling algorithm (TailScore):
- Inputs: tenant_id, weekly_request_volume, week_over_week_failure_delta, slice_failure_contribution, recent-token-length-distribution, tenant_privacy_flag.
- Output: sample_probability p in [0,1] and probe_type {live, synth, skip}.
- Heuristics (ordered):
  1. If tenant_privacy_flag == true -> probe_type = synth if allowed by compliance policy, else skip.
  2. Compute volume_score = min(1, log10(weekly_request_volume+1)/5).
  3. Compute failure_score = clamp((week_over_week_failure_delta / baseline_failure_rate), 0, 10).
  4. TailScore = 0.6*failure_score + 0.3*(1-volume_score) + 0.1*slice_failure_contribution.
  5. Map TailScore to p with piecewise mapping:
     - TailScore >= 6 -> p = min(0.5, base_p * (1 + (TailScore-6)/10))
     - 3 <= TailScore < 6 -> p = min(0.2, base_p * (1 + (TailScore-3)/8))
     - TailScore < 3 -> p = base_p * 0.3
  6. base_p is a system-wide floor (default 0.01) configurable via sampler-config.
- Probe_type selection: if weekly_request_volume < 100 and privacy flag allows, prefer synth with probability 0.85.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...] - Probe_type selection: if weekly_request_volume < 100 and privacy flag allows, prefer synth with probability 0.85.


Synth-probe generation rules:
- Input features used (non-identifying): request size histogram, token-length percentiles (10/50/90), endpoint mix distribution, common intent-tags (anonymized), response-length distribution.
- Privacy constraints: no raw user messages or PII allowed. All synth probes must be produced from aggregated and differentially-noised statistics (Laplace noise, epsilon=1.0 default).
- Probe schema (examples):
  - id: tspo-{tenant_id}-{YYYYMMDD}-{seq}
  - metadata: tenant_anon_bucket, endpoint_mix, token_profile, generated_seed_id
  - inputs: [priming_text_stub_1, priming_text_stub_2] (synthetic stubs derived from template library)
  - expected_metrics: p95_latency_range, coherence_score_target, rerank_accuracy_floor

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...]   - expected_metrics: p95_latency_range, coherence_score_target, rerank_accuracy_floor


Cost projection and budget gating (table):
| Item | Unit cost (tokens) | Expected units/week | Weekly cost est | Notes |
|------|--------------------:|---------------------:|----------------:|-------|
| Live-shadow probe | 0.00012 | 500 | $0.06 | full-fidelity model on Dedicated mesh
| Synth-probe (quant) | 0.00002 | 2,000 | $0.04 | quantized runtime via shadow-quant pool
| Batch eval compute | 0.00008 | 1,000 | $0.08 | nightly batch replay for aggregates
| TOTAL (example) | - | - | $0.18 | adjustable via orchestration rate-limits

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...] | TOTAL (example) | - | - | $0.18 | adjustable via orchestration rate-limits


Operational runbook (oncall action steps):
1. Alert trigger: Coverage SLO degrade (coverage < 95%) or projected monthly cost > 6% of eval budget.
2. Immediate actions:
   a. Open TSPO dashboard -> confirm affected tenant list and recent TailScore trends.
   b. If coverage degraded due to sampler regression, toggle sampler to "fallback-uniform" via sampler-config (CLI):
      - CLI: curl -X POST $SAMPLER_ADMIN/switch -d '{"mode":"fallback-uniform", "base_p":0.05}'
   c. If cost overrun predicted, reduce orchestrator throughput: PATCH /probe-orch/config {"max_rps": 0.1 * current}.
3. Escalation path: if toggles do not restore thresholds within 30 minutes, page ML-Platform oncall and include probe-registry manifest.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...] 3. Escalation path: if toggles do not restore thresholds within 30 minutes, page ML-Platform oncall and include probe-registry manifest.


Monitoring and alerts:
- Key metrics to expose in Redwood Console:
  - tspo.coverage_ratio (per-slice, per-tenant) — alert if < 0.95 for 24h window.
  - tspo.projected_monthly_cost — warn at 80% of cost SLO, critical at 100%.
  - tspo.synth_quality_index (BLEU/coherence proxies) — degrade if synths diverge from small live-sample baseline by > 12%
  - tspo.sampler_decision_tail_variance — alert if variance jumps > 3x week-over-week.
- Dashboards: Sampling heatmap (tenant vs TailScore), Orchestrator throttle slider, Probe artifact registry viewer.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...] - Dashboards: Sampling heatmap (tenant vs TailScore), Orchestrator throttle slider, Probe artifact registry viewer.


Rollout and safety gates:
1. Canary: pilot TSPO on a single service domain with 5% of tenants (mix of top, mid, tail) for 2 weeks.
2. Acceptance criteria for promotion:
   - No more than 0.5% increase in false-positive regression signals (measured by human-annotated checks)
   - Cost projection remains below 4% of monthly eval budget for the pilot domain
   - Synth-probe privacy audit completed by security (checklist item in Probe Registry record)
3. Gradual ramp: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% with at least 72 hours at each step to observe telemetry.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...] 3. Gradual ramp: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% with at least 72 hours at each step to observe telemetry.


Troubleshooting common failure modes:
- Symptom: sudden spike in sampler_decision_tail_variance after sampler release.
  - Likely cause: configuration parameter change in base_p or feature skew.
  - Mitigation: revert to previous sampler-config tag, and run offline calibration against historical week.
- Symptom: synth_quality_index drop > 15%.
  - Likely cause: template library drift or incorrect noise parameters.
  - Mitigation: run synth-audit tool to compare synths to small live holdout and roll forward updated templates.

title: Tenant-tail Sampling & Synthesized Probe Orchestrator Playbook
text:
[...]   - Mitigation: run synth-audit tool to compare synths to small live holdout and roll forward updated templates.


Ownership and contacts:
- Service owners:
  - sampler-tspo: maya.chen@redwood.com (owner)
  - synth-probe-gen: arjun.patel@redwood.com
  - probe-orch: sofia.morales@redwood.com
  - cost-estimator: infra-costing@redwood.com (team)
- Oncall rotation: ML-Platform Pager (see /confluence/eng-platform/runbooks/pager-rotations).

Appendix A: Example CLI snippets
- Trigger a dry-run synth generation for tenant 0xabc:
  - curl -X POST $SYNTH_ADMIN/generate -d '{"tenant_id":"0xabc","dry_run":true}'
- Queue a probe batch via orchestrator (YAML example):
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: ProbeBatch
    spec:
      tenant_bucket: tail-2025-q4
      batch_size: 200
      fidelity: quant
      schedule: "cron(0 3 * * MON)"

Appendix B: Sampler config (sampler-config.yaml) example
- base_p: 0.01
- max_live_fraction: 0.5
- synth_preference_threshold: 100
- noise_epsilon: 1.0
- cost_budget_percent: 0.06

Appendix C: Related artifacts and further reading
- Probe Registry: S3://probe-registry/tspo/ (index is internally accessible)
- Shadow mesh playbook: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/shadow-to-prod-eval-continuity-guidelines-2026
- Privacy audit checklist: /confluence/security-and-compliance/compliance-evidence/probe-privacy-checklist-2025

Change log:
- 2025-09-15 (Maya Chen): Initial draft and sampler algorithm proposal.
- 2025-11-12 (Arjun Patel): Added synth privacy noise defaults and CLI examples.
- 2026-01-20 (Sofia Morales): Finalized runbook steps, added cost projection table and rollout gates.

title: Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec
text:
Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec

Overview

This document defines the Tenant Journey Fabricator (SUJG — Synthetic User Journey Generator) and the downstream labeling pipeline used to produce high-quality, privacy-aware synthetic probes for long-tail coherence, context-fading, and multi-turn continuity evaluation. The SUJG is designed for per-tenant fidelity: it fabricates realistic multi-turn sessions that match a tenant's traffic fingerprint (intent distribution, mean-turn-length, context-repeats, entity density) without retaining PII. The output drives shadow evals, stress benches, and targeted annotation passes used during model onboarding, quantization validation, and regression triage.

Goals

- Provide reproducible, tenant-specific synthetic sessions that exercise coherence and memory failure modes at scale.
- Preserve privacy by design: no synthetic output should be reverse-engineerable to real user data.
- Produce labeled artifacts (gold/baseline, difficulty, failure-mode tags) that feed eval harnesses and automated triage.
- Support quantization-aware scenario generation so we can expose quant-induced degradation in realistic contexts.

Scope

Applies to: hosted, dedicated, and private evaluation pipelines. Not intended for generating production training data. This spec covers generation, anonymization, sampling weighting, labeling schema, storage/retention rules, and operational runbook for SUJG jobs.

Key Definitions

- Session: ordered list of turns (user + assistant) representing a multi-turn interaction.
- Fingerprint: statistical descriptor of tenant traffic (turn length distribution, intent mixture, token entropy, modal entities).
- Difficulty band: coarse label (easy/medium/hard) based on token ambiguity, required memory horizon, and branching factor.
- Quant scenario: parameter set for simulating quantized artifact (bitwidth, per-layer scheme, KV-cache perturbation profile).

Architecture Summary

1) Input: tenant fingerprint + seed set (non-sensitive templates) + sampling policy.
2) Generator: hybrid recipe combining template expansion, stochastic paraphrase model, and generative continuity seeding.
3) Anonymizer: differential-preserving redaction + entity-synthesis model that substitutes real tokens with synthetic analogs.
4) Augmentor: applies perturbations (typos, code formatting noise, multilingual fragments, context truncation) to produce robustness cases.
5) Labeler: heuristic + human-in-the-loop triage that assigns failure-mode tags and difficulty bands.
6) Export: artifacts published to eval-mesh and private shadow clusters per tenant.

Operational Config Table (example)

| Field | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| job_id | Unique scheduler id | sujg-2026-09-26-7321 |
| tenant_id | Tenant namespace | tenant-acme-corp |
| session_count | Total sessions to synthesize | 50,000 |
| mean_turns | Average turns per session | 6 |
| seed_templates | Template set label | helpdesk-FAQ-v2 |
| quant_scenarios | Quant scenario list | q8-hybrid,q4-neg10 |
| privacy_mode | anonymization level | strict (DP epsilon=0.5) |
| retention_days | Storage TTL | 30 |
| label_kickoff | Human review threshold | sample 0.5% or all failures |

Fingerprinting and Sampling Policy

- Fingerprints are computed from 30 days of tenant telemetry (requests sampled at 1%). Stored fingerprint fields: intent_histogram (top-20 intents), avg_token_length, pct_multilingual, mean_entity_count, context_reuse_rate.
- Sampling policy aims to allocate evaluation budget across: common (70%), rare-intent (20%), stress-scenarios (10%). Rare-intent and stress-scenarios are oversampled by a factor derived from the tenant's SLA-critical intent list.

Privacy and Anonymization

- All templates and seeds must be scrubbed of PII before consumption by SUJG. The anonymizer uses two steps: deterministic redaction (email, phone, SSN regex) then entity synthesis (replace with synthetic names, numerics, domains).
- Differential-privacy guardrail: aggregated outputs must satisfy DP target for tenant exposures. Default epsilon=0.5, delta=1e-6; configurable per-tenant by compliance.
- Local execution: for Private deployments the entire SUJG can run inside the customer's VPC. In-hosted mode, only fingerprint metadata (no content) leaves the tenant boundary.

Quantization-aware Scenarios

- Each session can be stamped with one or more quant scenario tags. The augmentor will simulate common quant failure modes: rounding noise on logits, KV-cache mismatch for long-context, softmax saturation drift.
- Example quant manifest (JSON snippet):

title: Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec
text:
[...] - Each session can be stamped with one or more quant scenario tags. The augmentor will simulate common quant failure modes: rounding noise on logits, KV-cache mismatch for long-context, softmax saturation drift.
- Example quant manifest (JSON snippet):

{"quant_scenario": "q8-hybrid", "per_layer_override": ["attn:8bit", "ffn:16bit"], "kv_noise_std": 0.002, "simulate_kv_eviction": true}

Labeling Schema

- Labels (single-turn and session-level): coherence_break, hallucination_entity, context_forgetting, repetition_loop, truncated_reasoning, formatting_loss, code_incorrectness.
- Difficulty score: 0-100 integer; derived deterministically from token ambiguity metrics + branching factor estimate.
- Human review buckets: auto-pass (confidence >= 0.95 & difficulty <= 20), sampled-check (0.6-0.95), full-review (confidence <= 0.6 or labeled failure).

Labeling Pipeline Steps

1) Auto-label: run deterministic heuristics + classifier model. Persist labels with confidence scores.
2) Sampling: pick sessions according to label_kickoff policy for human review. Use stratified sampling over difficulty bands.
3) Annotation: annotators use a guided UI showing session, model outputs (reference model + candidate model), quant scenario, and suggested labels.
4) Reconciliation: two annotators per item; disagreement sends to adjudicator.

Quality Gates and Promotion Criteria

- Per-tenant acceptance for model promotion to canary: degradation in labeled failure rate <= 1.5x baseline across top-10 intents, and median coherence index drop <= 6 points.
- Quant-fallback criteria: if q8 scenario increases critical intent failure rate by >3x, gate requires either rollout with reduced token budget OR fall back to higher-precision variant.

Integration Points

- Export format: NDJSON with fields [session_id, tenant_id, turns, labels, quant_scenario, difficulty, seed_template, fingerprint_tag]. Example line:

{"session_id":"s-0001","tenant_id":"tenant-acme","turns":[{"u":"How do I reset my password?","a":"To reset..."}],"labels":["coherence_pass"],"quant_scenario":"q8-hybrid","difficulty":12}

- Registry: SUJG jobs register with eval-mesh via /api/v1/sujg/register (POST). CLI example:

title: Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec
text:
[...] - Registry: SUJG jobs register with eval-mesh via /api/v1/sujg/register (POST). CLI example:


# register job
curl -X POST https://eval-mesh.internal/api/v1/sujg/register -H 'Authorization: Bearer $EVAL_KEY' -d '{"job_id":"sujg-2026-09-26-7321","tenant_id":"tenant-acme-corp"}'

Operational Runbook (operator steps)

1) Start a job
   - Submit fingerprint and seed_template set in the job UI or CLI. The system will estimate cost and required label budget.
2) Smoke test (minimum 100 sessions)
   - Validate anonymization (run SAMPLE_VALIDATION script)
   - Ensure no PII regex matches on export sample.
3) Ramp to full (progress 10% -> 50% -> 100%)
   - Monitor: generation throughput, label_confidence_histogram, failure_rate_by_intent.
4) Failure handling
   - If anonymizer flags >0.01% PII hits in sample, pause job and run redaction audit.
   - If human review disagreement > 20% in a day, reduce auto-label confidence threshold and re-run sampling.
5) Completion
   - Export NDJSON to secure bucket, notify eval-mesh, trigger shadow-scheduler to replay sessions against candidate models.

Monitoring and Alerts

- SLOs for SUJG job: job completion within SLA = 48h for up to 100k sessions (target 95th percentile).
- Key metrics to instrument: sessions_per_minute, anonymizer_pii_hits, label_confidence_median, human_disagreement_rate, quant_failure_delta_by_intent.
- Pager thresholds (example): anonymizer_pii_hits_rate > 0.0001 => pager to Privacy Eng; human_disagreement_rate > 0.2 => alert to QA leads.

Failure-mode Triage Template

1) Capture: job_id, sample session_id(s), tenant_id, quant_scenario, label set.
2) Reproduce: run session through local generator seeded with seed_id and fingerprint snapshot.
3) Isolate: toggle anonymizer, toggle augmentor perturbations, compare outputs.
4) Root cause hypotheses: seed leakage, paraphrase model hallucination, classification drift, quant-noise amplification.
5) Remediation: tighten redact regex, expand seed templates, retrain auto-label classifier, add quant-stability probe to eval harness.

Ownership and Contacts

- SUJG service owner: Asha Patel (eng-applied-ml). Pager: eng-applied-ml-oncall.
- Labeling operations: Sophie Nguyen (annotation-leads).
- Privacy guardrail contact: Daniel Park (security & privacy).

Appendices

title: Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec
text:
[...] Appendices


A. Seed template example (YAML)
---
- id: helpdesk-001
  description: common password reset flow
  user_prompts:
    - "I forgot my password"
    - "How do I change my password?"

title: Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec
text:
[...]     - "I forgot my password"
    - "How do I change my password?"

  expected_slots: [account, method]
---

B. Sample anonymizer regexes
- email: /[a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}/i
- phone: /\b(\+?\d{1,3}[- .]?)?(\d{3}[- .]?){2}\d{4}\b/

C. Quick CLI snippets

title: Tenant Journey Fabricator and Labeling Pipeline — SUJG Spec
section_header: run local generator for tenant fingerprint snapshot\python -m sujg.run --tenant tenant-acme-corp --seed helpdesk-001 --count 500 --out sample.ndjson
text:
[...] 
C. Quick CLI snippets

# run local generator for tenant fingerprint snapshot\python -m sujg.run --tenant tenant-acme-corp --seed helpdesk-001 --count 500 --out sample.ndjson

# validate anonymization sample
cat sample.ndjson | python -m sujg.validate_anonymization --pii-regex-list defaults.txt

D. Change log
- 2025-09-15: initial spec (Asha Patel).
- 2025-11-02: added DP guardrail and quant scenario schema (Marco Liu).
- 2026-02-04: revised labeling buckets and runbook thresholds (Sophie Nguyen).

Related pages and references

- Eval mesh schema reference: /applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/evaluator-interchange-format-and-federation-spec-2026
- Privacy & annotation retention: /applied-ml-and-evals/quality-guardrails/probe-privacy-budget-and-annotation-retention-playbook-2024

End of document

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec

Summary:
This operational spec defines the evaluation harness, probe generation, metric thresholds, and automated fallback procedures for deploying quantized model variants of the real-time rerank pipeline (service name: rerank-service). The goal is to allow safe promotion of quantized rerankers into production traffic with automated detection and graceful fallback to full-precision variants when quality or latency SLOs degrade.

Scope:
- Applies to rerank-service endpoints behind the inference gateway (route: /v1/rerank).
- Covers shadow evaluations, canary rollout, automated monitoring, and rollback.
- Includes quantized variants (int8/4-bit hybrid) and mixed-precision fallbacks.
- Excludes offline batch re-ranking jobs and non-rerank ranking pipelines.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] - Excludes offline batch re-ranking jobs and non-rerank ranking pipelines.


Key terminology:
- Probe: a synthetic or sampled request used to measure quality and latency.
- Shadow run: mirrored traffic evaluated against target model without affecting user responses.
- NDCG-delta: change in NDCG@10 versus baseline model.
- Quant-gatekeeper: the component that decides whether to route requests to quantized variant.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] - Quant-gatekeeper: the component that decides whether to route requests to quantized variant.


Stakeholders and ownership:
- Service owner: Search & Ranking team (primary oncall: @search-ranking-oncall).
- Eval harness owner: Applied ML (owner: Priya Nair).
- Observability: Eng Platform (dashboards: rerank/quantization-eval).
- Rollout approvals: Product + Applied ML for market-impacting models.

SLOs and acceptance criteria (must be met in shadow + canary phases):
- Latency SLO (production): P95 <= 50 ms for single-query rerank at 128 tokens, P99 <= 120 ms.
- Latency impact (quantized vs baseline): P95 delta <= +10 ms.
- Quality SLO: NDCG@10 delta >= -0.007 (i.e., loss must be < 0.007 absolute).
- Hallucination / safety sentinel: flagged rates must not increase by >0.2% absolute.
- Tokenization fidelity: OOV token rate change < 0.05%.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
section_header: Metric telemetry and thresholds to publish to the console
text:
[...] - Tokenization fidelity: OOV token rate change < 0.05%.


Metric telemetry and thresholds to publish to the console:
- ndcg_at_10, ndcg_delta, rerank_latency_ms_p95, rerank_latency_ms_p99, tokens_generated, hallucination_signal_rate, oov_rate, quant_error_rate.
- Alert rules (examples):
  - high-quality-delta: trigger if ndcg_delta < -0.007 for 5m rolling window AND sample-size >= 2000.
  - latency-regression: trigger if rerank_latency_ms_p95 increase > +12 ms vs baseline for 10m.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
section_header: Probe synthesis strategy (how we create test traffic)
text:
[...]   - latency-regression: trigger if rerank_latency_ms_p95 increase > +12 ms vs baseline for 10m.


Probe synthesis strategy (how we create test traffic):
1) Seeded shadow probes: sample 10% of production queries with user-consented telemetry flags and mirror to shadow-sequencer.
2) Synthetic perturbations: augment real queries with adversarial paraphrases and long-context extensions to stress KV-cache and quantization.
3) Rare-slice amplification: upsample low-frequency slices (locale=en-IN, conversational multi-turn) to reach minimum statistical power.

Probe composition and size guidance:
- Minimum per evaluation window: 5k probes across slices.
- Recommended slice allocation: 60% head queries, 30% medium-tail, 10% longtail/edgecases.
- Time windows: rolling 1h for latency, rolling 24h for quality stability before canary promotion.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] - Time windows: rolling 1h for latency, rolling 24h for quality stability before canary promotion.


Quant-sensitivity test matrix (example):
| Variant | Bits | KV cache enabled | Target SLO notes | Acceptance delta |
| Baseline | fp16 | yes | Reference | 0.0 |
| int8-ldq | 8 | yes | Expected minimal fidelity loss | ndcg >= -0.005 |
| hybrid-4+8 | mixed | yes | aggressive cost trade | ndcg >= -0.007 and latency <= +8ms |
| int4-edge | 4 | limited | experimental, only shadow | ndcg >= -0.01 and manual review |

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] | int4-edge | 4 | limited | experimental, only shadow | ndcg >= -0.01 and manual review |


Automated evaluation pipeline (high-level):
1) build-artifact: produce quantized model artifact and push to artifact repo (artifact tag: rerank/{model}:{quant-profile}:{sha}).
2) smoke-run: run local throughput + deterministic-output checks (tool: evaluator-sdk smoke-run).
3) shadow-run: replay probes via shadow-sequencer to quant variant. Collect metrics to telemetry-ingest and evaluator-db.
4) aggregation: compute slice-level metrics (ndcg, latency percentiles, hallucination rate).
5) gate decision: quant-gatekeeper consults rules engine and posts recommended action to rollout-service.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] 5) gate decision: quant-gatekeeper consults rules engine and posts recommended action to rollout-service.


Example CI job config (YAML):
job_name: rerank-quant-eval
cron: "*/30 * * * *"
steps:
  - name: download-artifact
    run: |
      aws s3 cp s3://artifacts/rerank/${MODEL_TAG}.tar.gz .
  - name: run-shadow
    run: |
      evaluator-sdk run --config configs/rerank-shadow.yaml --artifact ${MODEL_TAG} --out /tmp/results.json
  - name: upload-results
    run: |
      curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $EVAL_API_TOKEN" -F results=@/tmp/results.json https://eval-orchestrator.internal/api/results

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...]       curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $EVAL_API_TOKEN" -F results=@/tmp/results.json https://eval-orchestrator.internal/api/results


Rollout & canary process (step-by-step):
1) Shadow-only (48h): quant variant must pass shadow acceptance across all high-priority slices.
2) Micro-canary (0.5% traffic for 6h): route 0.5% user traffic through quant-gatekeeper with real-time comparison telemetry collected.
3) Ramp (0.5 -> 5 -> 20%): increase only if quality and latency remain within thresholds at each checkpoint (24h windows at each stage).
4) Full promotion: require stakeholder sign-off and documentation of observed deltas and mitigation plan.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] 4) Full promotion: require stakeholder sign-off and documentation of observed deltas and mitigation plan.


Automated fallback & rollback behavior:
- If alert high-quality-delta or latency-regression fires during canary/ramp, quant-gatekeeper will automatically route new requests to baseline for the affected region + model and open a high-priority incident.
- Fallback TTL: immediate re-route for 60 minutes; the rollout-service pauses ramps until manual review.
- For transient anomalies (blips <5m with <1% probes affected) hold and continue monitoring; do not auto-rollback unless condition persists for 10m and sample-size > 500.

Triage playbook for failures (oncall steps):
1) Acknowledge pager and pull up rerank/quantization-eval dashboard.
2) Confirm which slices triggered the alert and query sample IDs.
3) Run evaluator-sdk isolate --slice <slice_id> --artifact <artifact_tag> to reproduce locally.
4) If quant-error suspected, trigger auto-fallback (rerankctl fallback --artifact <artifact_tag> --region <region>).
5) Postmortem: capture root cause, affected slice, mitigation steps, and rollback timeline.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] 5) Postmortem: capture root cause, affected slice, mitigation steps, and rollback timeline.


Example CLI snippets (for oncall):
# Check recent ndcg deltas
curl -s "https://telemetry.internal/api/metrics?name=ndcg_delta&service=rerank-service&window=1h" | jq .
# Trigger a targeted fallback
rerankctl fallback --model rerank_v2026 --profile hybrid-4+8 --slice locale=en-IN

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
section_header: Evaluation report template (required before full promotion)
text:
[...] rerankctl fallback --model rerank_v2026 --profile hybrid-4+8 --slice locale=en-IN


Evaluation report template (required before full promotion):
- Artifact tag:
- Shadow window (start/end):
- Probe volume by slice:
- NDCG@10 baseline vs quant (table):
- Latency P95/P99 comparison (table):
- Safety sentinel comparison (table):
- Known limitations & edge cases:
- Recommended action (promote / widen shadow / rollback):

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] - Recommended action (promote / widen shadow / rollback):


Privacy and data handling:
- All shadow probes must follow the privacy budget policy: PII redaction enabled, retention 7d for probe payloads, annotation artifacts stored with encryption at rest.
- Rare-slice amplification must not include user-identifiable content — use redacted proxies or synthetic variants.

Testing checklist before on-call signoff:
- [ ] Smoke-run passed with deterministic checks.
- [ ] Shadow-run completed with required probe counts per slice.
- [ ] No slice-level ndcg_delta breaches for 24h.
- [ ] Latency regression within limits for 24h.
- [ ] Documentation of expected cost delta and deployment runbook.

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] - [ ] Documentation of expected cost delta and deployment runbook.


References and related pages:
- evaluator-sdk README: internal link (evaluator-sdk docs).
- Quant profile playbooks (see related_pages above).
- rerank-service runbook: sources/confluence/eng-serving-runtime/runbooks/rerank-service-runbook-2025.json

Appendix: quick troubleshooting matrix
- Symptom: increased ndcg_delta on locale=en-IN only -> Action: inspect tokenization differences between artifacts, run tokenization-diff tool, verify vocab alignment.
- Symptom: P95 latency increase -> Action: check batching behavior, KV-cache hit rate, and hardware scheduling (gpu-util).

title: Real-time Rerank Eval and Quant Fallback Operational Spec
text:
[...] - Symptom: P95 latency increase -> Action: check batching behavior, KV-cache hit rate, and hardware scheduling (gpu-util).


Contact list:
- Applied ML (owner): priya.nair@redwood.ai
- Search & Ranking oncall: search-ranking-oncall@redwood.ai
- Observability: monitoring@redwood.ai

Document changelog:
- 2026-02-08: Created initial spec (Priya Nair).
- 2026-03-01: Added automated fallback TTL rules and updated acceptance thresholds (reviewers: Ethan Chen, Maya Ortiz).

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments

Summary
-------
This playbook defines a standardized, runtime-sensitive evaluation contract used by product, model-onboarding, and serving teams to certify model variants across Redwood's three deployment tiers (Hosted API, Dedicated, Private). The goal is to provide a single, machine-actionable contract that specifies the set of probes, SLO thresholds, quantization compatibility checks, and regression-triage triggers required for promotion and rollback decisions.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] This playbook defines a standardized, runtime-sensitive evaluation contract used by product, model-onboarding, and serving teams to certify model variants across Redwood's three deployment tiers (Hosted API, Dedicated, Private). The goal is to provide a single, machine-actionable contract that specifies the set of probes, SLO thresholds, quantization compatibility checks, and regression-triage triggers required for promotion and rollback decisions.


Scope
-----
- Applies to new model versions and model-variant promotions (e.g., float32 -> int8, mixed-precision families).
- Covers integration points between eval harness, prompt catalog, quantization profiles, and runtime observability.
- Not a replacement for business-level acceptance tests (product signoff still required).

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] - Not a replacement for business-level acceptance tests (product signoff still required).


Definitions
-----------
- Fidelity contract: a declared mapping of quality metrics to deployment tiers and acceptable deltas versus baseline.
- Probe: a single evaluation item derived from the prompt catalog (single-shot or multi-turn).
- Latency slice: workload bucket defined by request size, functioncall usage, and p95/P99 latency sensitivity.
- Quant-compatibility test: synthetic and runtime checks that exercise tokenization, KV-cache behavior, and attention fallback for quantized kernels.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] - Quant-compatibility test: synthetic and runtime checks that exercise tokenization, KV-cache behavior, and attention fallback for quantized kernels.


SLO matrix (canonical)
----------------------
| Metric                          | Hosted (fastest path) | Dedicated (reserved) | Private (customer-managed) |
|---------------------------------|-----------------------:|---------------------:|---------------------------:|
| p50 generation latency (ms)     | < 30                 | < 45                | < 70                      |
| p95 generation latency (ms)     | < 120                | < 200               | < 350                     |
| Semantic fidelity (anchor F1)   | >= 0.92 vs baseline  | >= 0.90 vs baseline | >= 0.88 vs baseline       |
| Functioncall correctness (%)    | >= 99.0              | >= 98.0             | >= 96.5                   |
| Tokenization equivalence (score)| >= 0.995             | >= 0.99             | >= 0.98                   |
| Regression alert rate (per 100k)| <= 5                 | <= 10               | <= 25                     |

Notes: thresholds are default starting points. Product or customer contracts may tighten metrics. All numeric thresholds must be expressed in the release artifact (see artifact manifest section).

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] Notes: thresholds are default starting points. Product or customer contracts may tighten metrics. All numeric thresholds must be expressed in the release artifact (see artifact manifest section).


Artifact manifest (required keys)
---------------------------------
- model_id: string (e.g., rwd/gpt-xx-large)
- model_variant: string (e.g., fp16, int8-qdq)
- baseline_tag: string (reference model artifact used for delta comparisons)
- probe_suite_id: string (ID of the probe collection in the eval registry)
- quant_profile_id: string | "none"
- cutoff_latency_ms: integer (p95/cutoff used for routing)
- rollout_policy: one of [canary, staged, global]

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
section_header: Example JSON manifest snippet (for CI validation)
text:
[...] - rollout_policy: one of [canary, staged, global]


Example JSON manifest snippet (for CI validation):
```
{
  "model_id": "rwd-chat-7b",
  "model_variant": "int8-qdq-2025-03",
  "baseline_tag": "rwd-chat-7b-fp16-2025-02",
  "probe_suite_id": "probe-suite-stability-2025-02",
  "quant_profile_id": "qprofile-int8-lowloss-v2",
  "cutoff_latency_ms": "200",
  "rollout_policy": "staged"
}
```

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] ```


Probe selection and stratification
---------------------------------
1. Start from the canonical prompt catalog and derive three probe strata:
   - Core functional probes (30%): common intents and critical path examples.
   - Edge correctness probes (40%): long-tail prompts, numerics, sandboxed adversarial cases.
   - Latency/throughput probes (30%): synthetic high-concurrency and long-context scenarios.
2. Each probe in the suite must be tagged with: domain, complexity (len tokens), functioncall-usage, and privacy-sensitivity.
3. For quant-variant checks, include a replication of at least 12% of the core functional probes run under maximum context length.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] 3. For quant-variant checks, include a replication of at least 12% of the core functional probes run under maximum context length.


Quantization compatibility checklist
----------------------------------
- Tokenization equivalence: run tokenization roundtrip on 1000 representative examples; mean token error <= 0.5%.
- KV cache checksum parity: deterministic checksum delta under shadow replay < 0.1% of tokens.
- Attention fallback coverage: run failure-mode injection where numerical instabilities are forced; verify safe-fallback triggers exist.
- Output entropy drift: KL divergence vs baseline embeddings distribution < 0.06.
- Functioncall schema conformance: functioncall outputs must validate against declared JSON schemas for > 99% of probes.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] - Functioncall schema conformance: functioncall outputs must validate against declared JSON schemas for > 99% of probes.


Eval harness integration pattern
-------------------------------
- CI Step A (static checks): validate artifact manifest, probe suite registration, and quant profile tagging. Failure blocks pipeline.
- CI Step B (shadow-run): run probe suite against baseline and candidate for deterministic probes; collect diff metrics.
- CI Step C (runtime sim): use serving-simulation harness to emulate mixed-latency slices and batch sizes; assert that latency/SLOs meet tier targets.
- Telemetry export: produce an evaluation report in NDJSON with per-probe metrics, environment hash, and runtime fingerprint.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] - Telemetry export: produce an evaluation report in NDJSON with per-probe metrics, environment hash, and runtime fingerprint.


Promotion and rollout decision matrix
------------------------------------
- Pass/Fail rules are evaluated in order:
  1) Static manifest & probe registration (must pass)
  2) Semantic fidelity: candidate delta <= allowed budget for target tier
  3) Latency: candidate p95 <= cutoff_latency_ms in artifact manifest
  4) Quant-compatibility: all quant checks pass for quantized variants
  5) Canary smoke: limited production canary must show no regression alerts over 48h
- If any critical check fails, the model is rejected for that tier. Non-critical failures can be assigned to a mitigation plan with a measured debt budget.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] - If any critical check fails, the model is rejected for that tier. Non-critical failures can be assigned to a mitigation plan with a measured debt budget.


Regression triage playbook (fast path)
-------------------------------------
1. Automated detection: alert triggered when regression alert rate > threshold in SLO matrix.
2. Fast capture: run per-probe diffs using the NDJSON reports captured during rollout; group by slice tags (domain, complexity).
3. Hypothesis generation: triage owner (oncall/eval-owner) fills triage template with top 3 hypotheses: tokenization, quant rounding, prompt sensitivity.
4. Targeted rollback: if top-priority slice shows core functionality regression (semantic fidelity drop > 3%), trigger immediate rollback for affected traffic tier.
5. Investigate offline: schedule a regression-bisect run against commit DAG + quant profile variance.

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
[...] 5. Investigate offline: schedule a regression-bisect run against commit DAG + quant profile variance.


Operational runbook (for SREs and oncall)
-----------------------------------------
- Immediate checks (first 15 min):
  - Validate artifact manifest used by the active pool. CLI: rwdctl model describe --id <model_id> --variant <model_variant>
  - Check most recent eval-report NDJSON in /eval-reports/<probe_suite_id>/latest
  - Confirm p95 latency on dashboard (model-route-level) and compare to artifact cutoff_latency_ms
- Escalation steps (15-60 min):
  - If regression confirmed, notify product and applied-ml teams, attach probe-sample list and NDJSON diffs.
  - Kick off regression-bisect job: jobs/regress-bisect --start-commit X --end-commit Y --probes probe-list.json

title: Runtime-sensitive eval contract for multi-tier deployments
text:
