title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol

Summary
-------
This document defines the protocol and runbook for capturing, replaying, and triaging nondeterministic behavior observed in evaluation harness runs. It codifies artifact capture, deterministic-mode execution, tolerance thresholds, and escalation steps so engineering and applied ML teams can reliably diagnose and reduce stochastic divergence between runs.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] This document defines the protocol and runbook for capturing, replaying, and triaging nondeterministic behavior observed in evaluation harness runs. It codifies artifact capture, deterministic-mode execution, tolerance thresholds, and escalation steps so engineering and applied ML teams can reliably diagnose and reduce stochastic divergence between runs.


Scope
-----
- Applies to all automated and manual eval harness runs used for production model certification, A/B tests, and regression detection.
- Covers sources of nondeterminism including sampling configs (temperature, top_k/top_p), tokenizer differences, runtime kernel scheduling, batch coalescing, KV cache state, and hardware/driver variance.
- Does NOT cover data-collection-level label noise or human annotation variance (see separate QA guidelines).

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - Does NOT cover data-collection-level label noise or human annotation variance (see separate QA guidelines).


Goals
-----
- Make a failing or flaky eval reproducible within the harness >= 90% of the time using captured artifacts.
- Provide a consistent artifact set that allows replays across environments (dev -> staging -> dedicated).
- Reduce actionable nondeterminism by 30% year-over-year via instrumentation and targeted fixes.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - Reduce actionable nondeterminism by 30% year-over-year via instrumentation and targeted fixes.


Terminology
-----------
- Replay bundle: a single tarball containing request payloads, RNG seeds, tokenizer checksums, runtime snapshot, model binary id, and KV cache dump where applicable.
- Deterministic mode: runtime mode where sampling is disabled (greedy / beam) or sampling is run with explicit seed control and kernel pinning.
- Divergence: measurable difference between responses (token-level edit distance, log-prob delta, or evaluation score delta).

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
section_header: Collect the following for every eval run flagged as flaky or when running a determinism sweep
text:
[...] - Divergence: measurable difference between responses (token-level edit distance, log-prob delta, or evaluation score delta).


Artifact capture (what to collect)
--------------------------------
Collect the following for every eval run flagged as flaky or when running a determinism sweep: 
- run_metadata.json (schema below)
- request_payloads.ndjson (one request per line, include full prompt, headers, and model_version)
- tokenizer_snapshot.txt (tokenizer version + serialized vocab checksum)
- rng_state.bin (binary RNG state for CPU/GPU PRNGs if accessible) or seed log entry when binary not available
- kv_cache_<shard>.bin (kv cache snapshot for the session; optional and gated by storage policy)
- runtime_snapshot.txt (container image id, kernel id, CUDA/cuDNN driver versions, pinned kernel flags, environment variables relevant to runtime)
- batching_log.jsonl (log of batch composition and timing for the run)
- model_artifact_id.txt (release tag + checksum)
- system_hardware_checksum.txt (GPU model + BIOS + minor revision string)

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
section_header: Config reference table (keys accepted by harness)
text:
[...] - system_hardware_checksum.txt (GPU model + BIOS + minor revision string)


Example run_metadata.json (single-line example)
------------------------------------------------
{"run_id": "eval-2025-03-12-2048", "env": "staging", "model": "redwood-7b-v1", "deterministic_mode": false, "seed": null, "sampling_policy": "top_p=0.95", "max_tokens": 256}

Config reference table (keys accepted by harness)
------------------------------------------------
| key | type | description | default |
|-----|------|-------------|---------|
| deterministic_mode | boolean | Force non-sampling generation or enable seeded sampling pathway | false |
| seed | integer | PRNG seed for sampling pathways; applied to CPU/GPU PRNGs when available | null |
| sampling_policy | string | JSON-encoded sampling config (temperature, top_k, top_p) | \"{\"temperature\":1.0}\" |
| runtime_snapshot | string | path to runtime_snapshot.txt to force replay env | current |
| capture_kv_cache | boolean | whether to snapshot KV cache for the session | false |
| batch_strategy | string | \"auto\" | \"auto\" |
| tolerance_threshold | string | metric-specific threshold for acceptable divergence | \"metric:0.03\" |

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] | tolerance_threshold | string | metric-specific threshold for acceptable divergence | \"metric:0.03\" |


Protocol: capture -> replay -> classify
-------------------------------------
1) Capture: When a run is flagged (automated check or manual), immediately tag run as \"capture_required\". The harness should persist all artifacts above to S3 under /eval-replays/<run_id>. A capture job must finish within 30s of detection to maximize fidelity.

2) Replay (local/staging):
   a) Run the replay harness with the same request_payloads and runtime_snapshot. By default, start in deterministic_mode=true and seed=42.
   b) If deterministic-mode reproduces the issue, classify as \"sampling-agnostic\". Proceed to kernel/runtime triage.
   c) If deterministic-mode does NOT reproduce, run seeded sampling with the original sampling_policy and try seed enumeration (see Seed Enumeration below) up to 1,000 seeds or 1 hour wall time.
   d) If seeded sampling finds a match, classify as \"seed-sensitive\". Record the matching seed in the replay bundle metadata.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...]    d) If seeded sampling finds a match, classify as \"seed-sensitive\". Record the matching seed in the replay bundle metadata.


Seed Enumeration (when to use)
-----------------------------
- Use when nondeterminism is suspected to be due to sampling and the failure signature is easily comparable (exact token sequence or evaluation score threshold).
- Enumeration approach: deterministic-mode -> seeded sampling sweep with offset strategy (seed = base_seed + offset) in parallel shards. Stop early when match found. Log seeds tested in seeds_tested.log.

Classification of failures
--------------------------
- Deterministic mismatch: reproduced under deterministic_mode -> root causes: tokenizer drift, model binary mismatch, floating-point math/precision.
- Seed-sensitive: reproducible only under specific seeds -> root causes: sampling edge cases, prompt-sensitive sampling boundaries.
- Environment-dependent: reproduces only when runtime_snapshot differs -> root causes: driver/kernel/runtime flags, pinned kernels.
- Batch-induced: only reproducible when batch composition matches original batching_log -> root causes: KV cache reads/writes, request interleaving, race conditions.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - Batch-induced: only reproducible when batch composition matches original batching_log -> root causes: KV cache reads/writes, request interleaving, race conditions.


Runbook: Reproduce a failing eval
--------------------------------
1) Fetch the replay bundle: aws s3 cp s3://redwood-eval-replays/<run_id>/bundle.tar.gz ./ && tar xzf bundle.tar.gz
2) Inspect run_metadata.json and batching_log.jsonl for obvious mismatches.
3) Start a replay node with runtime snapshot: eval-harness --runtime-snapshot=runtime_snapshot.txt --replay-dir=./<run_id>
4) First attempt: eval-harness --deterministic-mode=true --requests=request_payloads.ndjson --model-id=$(cat model_artifact_id.txt)
5) If step 4 fails to reproduce, run seeded sweep: eval-harness --seed-sweep --base-seed=1000 --parallel-shards=8 --requests=request_payloads.ndjson
6) If reproduction occurs, create an issue with classification, attach matching seed (if any), and link to relevant telemetry (traces, GPU logs).

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] 6) If reproduction occurs, create an issue with classification, attach matching seed (if any), and link to relevant telemetry (traces, GPU logs).


Mitigation and rollback guidance
--------------------------------
- If classification shows tokenizer drift: pin tokenizer version in model onboarding and trigger a republish of tokenization artifacts. Notify model-onboarding team.
- If environmental: pin runtime image + driver and roll forward to stable image across environments. Consider gating on canary traffic for 24h.
- If batch-induced: enable strict batch boundaries for the failing route or force KV cache isolation for the short term.
- If seed-sensitive but low impact: add seed-stability tests to the eval suite for the prompt in question. If high impact, consider adjusting sampling_policy (reduce temperature or clamp top_k).

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - If seed-sensitive but low impact: add seed-stability tests to the eval suite for the prompt in question. If high impact, consider adjusting sampling_policy (reduce temperature or clamp top_k).


Acceptance criteria and SLOs
---------------------------
- Reproducible replay success rate: target >= 90% for high-confidence failures within 1 hour of analysis.
- False-positive divergence alarms: < 2% of daily alerts.
- Automated capture completion: 99.5% of flagged runs must have a complete replay bundle within 30s.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - Automated capture completion: 99.5% of flagged runs must have a complete replay bundle within 30s.


Instrumentation and telemetry requirements
-----------------------------------------
- All eval harness runs must log batch composition (request ids + sample offsets) and streaming chunk boundaries.
- RNG seeds (or seed generation method) must be logged to run_metadata.json for every run.
- Tokenizer version and checksum must be recorded and surfaced in the Console for each model version.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - Tokenizer version and checksum must be recorded and surfaced in the Console for each model version.


Ownership and escalation
------------------------
- Primary owner: Eval Harness Team (eng-applied-ml). On-call: eval-harness-oncall rotation.
- Escalation path: Eval Harness -> Serving Runtime -> Model Onboarding -> Security (if artifacts contain customer PII).
- Contact: evan.brooks@redwood.ai for process questions; use #eval-replay Slack channel for coordination.

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...] - Contact: evan.brooks@redwood.ai for process questions; use #eval-replay Slack channel for coordination.


Appendix: Practical examples
---------------------------
- CLI: force deterministic replay
  eval-harness --replay-dir=./eval-2025-03-12-2048 --deterministic-mode=true --model-id=$(cat model_artifact_id.txt)

- YAML eval job snippet:
  job_id: replay-2025-03-12
  deterministic_mode: true
  seed: 42
  requests: request_payloads.ndjson
  runtime_snapshot: runtime_snapshot.txt
  capture_kv_cache: true

title: Stochastic Eval Replay and Determinism Protocol
text:
[...]   capture_kv_cache: true


References and related pages
----------------------------
- Serving simulation harness (simulation guidelines) -- /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/serving-simulation-eval-harness-2026
- KV cache quant stability audit -- /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/quantization-profiles/kv-cache-quant-stability-audit-playbook-2025

Revision history
----------------
- 2025-03-12: Initial draft filed (Evan Brooks).
- 2025-04-08: Added seed enumeration strategy and example CLI (Sarah Liu review).
- 2025-06-01: Formalized capture schema and SLO targets; moved to published status (Mateo Alvarez review).

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook

Summary:
This document specifies the Quantized Replay Orchestrator (QRO): an eval-harness component used to replay production or synthetic traffic against quantized model variants in a controlled, auditable manner. The QRO is intended for: pre-promotion safety checks, longtail stability checks for quantized kernels, and regression triage when inference drift is suspected after quantization or runtime changes.

Goals and non-goals:
- Goals:
  - Provide deterministic replay behavior for token-level and example-level traces.
  - Measure delta metrics (quality, latency, token cost) between float baseline and quantized variants.
  - Surface regressions that are functionally actionable (prompt-level failure modes and statistically significant output shifts).
- Non-goals:
  - This is not a replacement for live A/B canaries; QRO is a controlled offline/nearline analysis tool.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   - This is not a replacement for live A/B canaries; QRO is a controlled offline/nearline analysis tool.


Scope and When to Run:
- Scope: models served by Redwood serving runtime (gpt-family and encoder-decoder variants) running in hosted, dedicated, or private deployments.
- Run triggers (choose one or more):
  1. Before promoting a quantized build to staging/dedicated pools.
  2. After kernel or runtime changes that affect attention/KV cache.
  3. On regression alerts flagged by continuous evals or field reports.
  4. Weekly longtail scans on a stratified subset of traffic.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   4. Weekly longtail scans on a stratified subset of traffic.


Architecture (text diagram):
- Components:
  - Ingress: replay-worker fleet (containerized) that pulls traces from Replay Store.
  - Replay Store: S3-backed event store with schema-ed traces (request metadata, tokenization, deterministic RNG seed).
  - Controller: qro-controller (orchestrates batches, model mapping, quant profiles).
  - Normalizer: request normalizer that ensures identical tokenization and prefix padding between baseline and variant.
  - Metrics Collector: emits QA metrics to /internal/metric/v1 and detailed artifact storage to Replay Store (per-example diff blobs).
  - Comparator: post-process job that computes deltas and significance tests.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   - Comparator: post-process job that computes deltas and significance tests.


Operational SLOs and Targets:
- Correctness: replay determinism >= 99.95% (same tokenized input and RNG seed produces matching deterministic non-deterministic ops when configured).
- Coverage: scheduled scan should process >= 95% of target trace sample within target window (default 24h for weekly scans).
- Latency: single-example replay time not an SLO (batch throughput target: 300 examples/s per 8xA100 equivalent pool when running non-streaming scoring).
- Alert thresholds (comparator outputs):
  - Mean lexical divergence (BLEU-like) drop > 3.0 pts -> P1 review.
  - Mean embedding cosine drop > 0.04 -> P1 review if correlated with user-reported regressions.
  - Median per-token latency increase > 20ms -> perf investigation (P2).

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
section_header: Data and Trace Format (table represented in text)
text:
[...]   - Median per-token latency increase > 20ms -> perf investigation (P2).


Data and Trace Format (table represented in text):
- Required fields in each trace record (newline-separated table-like block):
  - trace_id: string (uuid)
  - timestamp_utc: ISO8601
  - client_id: string (anonymized)
  - model_baseline: string (model:version)
  - model_variant: string (quantized profile id)
  - prompt: raw text
  - token_ids: list<int> (BPE ids) encoded as comma-separated string
  - seed: int
  - metadata: JSON-stringified map (app_version, region, route)
  - expected_answer (optional): text for labeled prompts

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   - expected_answer (optional): text for labeled prompts


Configuration example (YAML snippet):
---
controller: 
  concurrency: 64
  batch_size: 8
  retry_policy: 
    max_retries: 2
    backoff_ms: 500
model_mapping: 
  baseline: gpt-3.5-infer-float:v1
  variants: [gpt-3.5-infer-q8:profile-2026-01, gpt-3.5-infer-q4:profile-2026-01]
sampling: 
  strategy: stratified
  strata: ["high_latency_routes","low_latency_routes","rare_intents"]
  sample_count: 10000
artifact_paths: 
  replay_store: s3://redwood-replay-store/2026/qro/

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...] artifact_paths: 
  replay_store: s3://redwood-replay-store/2026/qro/

  diffs: s3://redwood-replay-diffs/
---

Example CLI commands:
- Submit a run: 
  ./qro submit --config configs/qro-staging.yaml --run-id qro-2026-02-03-staging
- Stream metrics (dev):
  ./qro tail-metrics --run-id qro-2026-02-03-staging | jq .
- Collect artifacts locally: 
  aws s3 sync s3://redwood-replay-diffs/qro-2026-02-03-staging ./artifacts

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   aws s3 sync s3://redwood-replay-diffs/qro-2026-02-03-staging ./artifacts


Comparator outputs and required follow-up:
- Output files produced per run (artifact naming):
  - summary.json: high-level statistics and pass/fail flags
  - per-example-diffs/{trace_id}.ndjson: token alignment and lexical diffs
  - statistical-report.html: auto-generated report with charts and significance tests
- Pass/fail rules (automated gate):
  1. Block promotion if ANY of the following are true:
     - >0.5% of labeled prompts flip classification label
     - BLEU drop > 3.0 pts and p-value < 0.01
     - Embedding drift above threshold and median confidence drop > 0.05
  2. If blocked, assign to regression-triage queue (see triage checklist).

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   2. If blocked, assign to regression-triage queue (see triage checklist).


Triage checklist (for humans):
1. Review summary.json and top 50 per-example diffs by severity.
2. Check tokenization mismatches: ensure normalizer used identical token vocab.
3. Run deterministic replays for failing examples with higher verbosity: 
   ./qro inspect --trace-id <trace_id> --verbosity debug --runs 10
4. Check for runtime-only causes: kernel changes, attention precision flags, and KV cache boundary conditions.
5. If quantization is implicated, run float vs quant with identical seed and compare logits: 
   python tools/compare_logits.py --float ./artifacts/float_logits --quant ./artifacts/quant_logits --trace-id <id>
6. If failure is correlated with specific prompt patterns, add to prompt-catalog bugset and tag for future evals.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
section_header: Integration with model onboarding and quality guardrails
text:
[...] 6. If failure is correlated with specific prompt patterns, add to prompt-catalog bugset and tag for future evals.


Integration with model onboarding and quality guardrails:
- This run should be attached to the model promotion PR as an artifact. Link the summary.json under the PR checklist item: "QRO run attached and PASS/FAIL status".
- For Private deployments, export only aggregated metrics (no identifiable prompt text) unless customer consent is present. Use DP sanitizer: tools/dp_sanitizer --input artifacts --output s3://redwood-privacy-archive/

Quantization-specific considerations:
- deterministic rounding differences: enable "stable-quant" mode when comparing small-decimal logits.
- KV caching: ensure replay worker simulates the exact cache warmup pattern (prefix lengths, streaming vs non-streaming).
- Mixed-precision fallbacks: compare variant with and without mixed-precision to isolate kernel regressions.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...] - Mixed-precision fallbacks: compare variant with and without mixed-precision to isolate kernel regressions.


Owner and escalation paths:
- QRO owners: Applied ML - Eval Infrastructure team (primary: @applied-ml-eval).
- On-call escalation: first contact: Marcus Li (applied-ml-eval oncall), then Eng SRE rotation, then Runtime team lead.
- Pager conditions: failed scheduled run (>= 95% sample not processed), or comparator encountering internal errors (exception rate > 5%).

Run cadence and reporting:
- Ad-hoc for promotions, weekly longtail scans (by default Saturdays 02:00 UTC), and immediate runs after kernel merges affecting quant routines.
- Reports are published to the Eval Dashboard under: Evaluations > QRO > last-30-days. The summary.json is auto-imported and emailed to model owners.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...] - Reports are published to the Eval Dashboard under: Evaluations > QRO > last-30-days. The summary.json is auto-imported and emailed to model owners.


Troubleshooting (short playbook):
- Problem: Many tokenization mismatches
  - Check tokenizer version in controller config (tokenizer_version). Re-run with tokenizer snapshot.
- Problem: Non-deterministic diffs across repeated runs
  - Verify RNG seed handling; set seed_override in config to force stable runs.
  - Confirm runtime flags: disable async-sampling and approximate top-k in the replay job.
- Problem: Large embedding drift for non-semantic prompts
  - Confirm embedding model version match and remove whitespace-normalization differences.

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...]   - Confirm embedding model version match and remove whitespace-normalization differences.


Privacy and compliance notes:
- Do not include un-sanitized customer-identifiable content in public artifacts. For enterprise customers, follow the Private deployment export policy and obtain Data Export Approval ticket.

Appendix A: Example minimal config (inline JSON)
{ 
  "run_id": "qro-minimal-2026-02-03",
  "baseline": "gpt-3.5-infer-float:v1",
  "variants": ["gpt-3.5-infer-q8:profile-2026-01"],
  "sample_count": 1000,
  "stratify_by": "route",
  "artifact_bucket": "s3://redwood-replay-diffs/qro-minimal-2026-02-03"
}

Appendix B: Quick link references:
- Quant-profile synthesis playbook: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/quantization-profiles/quant-profile-synthesis-and-safety-playbook-2024
- Shadow deployment eval playbook: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/shadow-deployment-eval-playbook-for-private-and-dedicated-2026
- Regression triage handbook: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/regression-triage/causal-regression-investigation-handbook-2026

title: Quantized Replay Orchestrator: Spec and Runbook
text:
[...] - Regression triage handbook: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/regression-triage/causal-regression-investigation-handbook-2026
